operator_view: The Batch 1 remediation plan is structurally sound with well-defined module boundaries and explicit interface contracts. Six planned contracts address the two manual fallback causes (doc drift, diff overflow) plus the challenger non-determinism observed. Three items require clarification before execution: (1) append_only vs targeted_patch mode boundary in doc rewrite drift guard, (2) deterministic child chunk ordering for recursive split, and (3) atomicity contract for partial rollback scenarios in recovery. Once these are clarified, the plan is ready for Batch 2 execution with the explicit acceptance criteria (5/5 cycles, 0 manual interventions, challenger trigger observed >=1).

claims.statement: Task spec lint contract is well-defined with explicit fail-closed pre-flight validation for required fields (allowed_paths, api_shape, doc_mutation_mode, max_expected_diff_lines) and path canonicalization. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Doc rewrite drift guard is positioned inside task_spec_lint.py as explicit intent validator, which is architecturally appropriate for pre-run fail-closed enforcement. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Deterministic challenger validation uses fixed known-bad fixture with clear pass/fail conditions tied to rework trigger observation. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Steward diff preflight enforces 300-line cap with auto-split orchestration and SPLIT_FAIL hard stop on recursive guard failure. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Attempt ledger uses atomic write semantics (temp file + fsync + atomic rename) with CLEAN_FAIL hard gate. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Recovery runbook requirement for rollback/retry preserves repo-clean and no-orphans invariants. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Module boundaries are clear: linting (preflight), challenger (fixture gate), steward (diff preflight), loop (ledger commit). REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Interface between diff_preflight and steward is explicit: block steward invocation when split plan is missing for oversized diffs. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
[ASSUMPTION] The challenger deterministic fixture validation may introduce nondeterminism if the fixture content varies across batches; verification needed that fixture is versioned and invariant.

Doc rewrite drift guard logic in task_spec_lint.py must distinguish append_only vs targeted_patch modes; if implementation conflates these, append-only docs could still drift. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Recursive split guard requires deterministic child chunk ordering; if hunk ordering is nondeterministic, SPLIT_FAIL may trigger inconsistently. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Atomic ledger contract does not specify what happens if commit succeeds but fsync-based durability guarantee fails (kernel-level issue); CLEAN_FAIL may not trigger appropriately. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
[ASSUMPTION] Recovery runbook atomicity during rollback is not specified—if rollback partially fails, repo-clean invariant may be violated.

Path canonicalization must handle symlinks that resolve outside allowed_paths after canonicalization; current spec mentions symlink escape guard but implementation detail is sparse. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Add explicit validation in task_spec_lint.py that doc_mutation_mode=append_only rejects any diff touching more than one contiguous section of a target file. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Specify stable sort order (e.g., lexicographic) for recursive split guard child chunks to guarantee deterministic SPLIT_FAIL behavior. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Clarify in attempt_ledger_commit.py that CLEAN_FAIL triggers on rename failure OR commit failure, with explicit recovery path. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Add versioning metadata to challenger known-bad fixture to guarantee deterministic validation across batches. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Document in CLEAN_FAIL_Recovery_Runbook.md the atomicity contract for partial rollback scenarios. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Clarify symlink resolution order in task_spec_lint.py: canonicalize first, then validate against allowed_paths after all symlink resolution. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

operator_view: Batch 1 remediation plan is governance-compliant and ready for Batch 2 execution. The implementation contracts address all three manual fallback causes: (1) doc rewrite drift guard via task_spec_lint.py enforces targeted_patch mode for INDEX/protocol docs; (2) steward diff preflight with recursive split guard prevents 300-line cap overflow; (3) deterministic challenger fixture validation ensures known-bad artifacts trigger rework. Atomic ledger continuity with CLEAN_FAIL gate preserves repo-clean invariant. No modifications to protected governance paths detected. All acceptance criteria (5/5 cycles, 0 manual interventions, >=1 challenger fixture trigger, no diff-cap blocks, no doc drift, repo-clean preservation) are addressable through the proposed contracts.

{'Doc rewrite drift guard enforces targeted_patch mode for INDEX/protocol docs, preventing the append-to-whole-file drift that caused Batch 1 manual fallback REF': 'AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts'}

No blocking issues identified. Version metadata should be added to planned contracts during implementation for forward compatibility.

claims.statement: Plan addresses all three Batch 1 failure modes with fail-closed mechanisms: doc rewrite drift guard in task_spec_lint.py, diff cap preflight + split in diff_preflight.py, deterministic challenger fixture gate. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Recursive split guard with SPLIT_FAIL hard-stop prevents unbounded diff propagation to steward, addressing the 340-line overflow observed in Batch 1. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
